metadata_source: local apt-cache package and source metadata, installed man pages, installed headers, installed binaries, and installed package file lists from an Ubuntu 24.04.3 system configured against the Ubuntu 24.04 repositories

selection_scope.goal: Curate a diverse, non-exhaustive set of Ubuntu 24.04 packages whose build or runtime behavior directly depends on libbz2.
selection_policy: Prefer direct binary-package dependencies on libbz2-1.0 and direct source-package Build-Depends on libbz2-dev.
Prefer packages from different domains: compression tools, package management, language runtimes and bindings, databases, cryptography, multimedia, fonts, and archive handling.
Describe the runtime behavior that uses libbz2 instead of only restating that the package depends on it.
notes: This file is intentionally representative rather than exhaustive.
Most selected packages have both a direct runtime dependency on libbz2-1.0 and a direct build dependency on libbz2-dev.
The bzip2 utilities package is included because the binary package itself directly depends on libbz2-1.0 even though the source package does not Build-Depend on libbz2-dev.

dependents.runtime_functionality: APT's core package-management library can acquire and decompress bzip2-compressed repository metadata through the configured bz2 compression type.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata: libapt-pkg6.0t64 depends on libbz2-1.0.
Ubuntu source metadata: apt Build-Depends on libbz2-dev.
The shipped file /usr/share/doc/apt/examples/configure-index declares bz2 under Acquire::CompressionTypes and includes it in the preferred compression order.

dependents.runtime_functionality: Provides the bzip2, bunzip2, and bzcat utilities for creating and reading .bz2-compressed files.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata: bzip2 depends on libbz2-1.0.
Ubuntu package description identifies it as the high-quality block-sorting file compressor utilities package.

dependents.runtime_functionality: Provides Python's bz2 module and the _bz2 extension for reading and writing bzip2-compressed data streams and files.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata: libpython3.12-stdlib depends on libbz2-1.0.
Ubuntu source metadata: python3.12 Build-Depends on libbz2-dev.
Installed package files include /usr/lib/python3.12/bz2.py and /usr/lib/python3.12/lib-dynload/_bz2.cpython-312-x86_64-linux-gnu.so.

dependents.runtime_functionality: Adds PHP functions for working with bzip2-compressed data and files.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata: php8.3-bz2 depends on libbz2-1.0.
Ubuntu source metadata: php8.3 Build-Depends on libbz2-dev.
Ubuntu package description explicitly says this package provides the bzip2 module for PHP.

dependents.runtime_functionality: Exposes the Bzip2 compression algorithm directly to Pike programs.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata: pike8.0-bzip2 depends on libbz2-1.0.
Ubuntu source metadata: pike8.0 Build-Depends on libbz2-dev.
Ubuntu package description says the module enables Pike programs to use the Bzip2 compression algorithm.

dependents.runtime_functionality: Supplies Perl code with a low-level interface to bzip2 compression and decompression primitives.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata: libcompress-raw-bzip2-perl depends on libbz2-1.0.
Ubuntu source metadata: libcompress-raw-bzip2-perl Build-Depends on libbz2-dev.
Ubuntu package description explicitly calls it a low-level interface to the bzip2 compression library for Perl.

dependents.runtime_functionality: Adds BZip2 compression support for MariaDB server and storage-engine paths such as InnoDB and Mroonga.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata: mariadb-plugin-provider-bzip2 depends on libbz2-1.0.
Ubuntu source metadata: mariadb Build-Depends on libbz2-dev.
Ubuntu package description explicitly says the plugin provides BZip2 compression support in the server and storage engines and notes that it affects InnoDB and Mroonga.

dependents.runtime_functionality: Supports OpenPGP payload compression with the bzip2 algorithm, including compression-level and low-memory decompression options.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata: gpg depends on libbz2-1.0.
Ubuntu source metadata: gnupg2 Build-Depends on libbz2-dev.
The gpg man page documents --compress-algo bzip2, --bzip2-compress-level, and --bzip2-decompress-lowmem.

dependents.runtime_functionality: Can create ZIP archives whose entries use the bzip2 compression method, for example with zip -Z bzip2.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata: zip depends on libbz2-1.0.
Ubuntu source metadata: zip Build-Depends on libbz2-dev.
The zip man page says zip supports bzip2 compression when built with the bzip2 library and documents zip -Z bzip2.

dependents.runtime_functionality: Can extract ZIP archives whose members use the bzip2 compression method.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata: unzip depends on libbz2-1.0.
Ubuntu source metadata: unzip Build-Depends on libbz2-dev.
unzip -v reports the USE_BZIP2 build option and the linked bzip2 library version.

dependents.runtime_functionality: Reads and writes bzip2-compressed archive streams, including the bzip2 filter paths used by libarchive-based tools and applications.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata: libarchive13t64 depends on libbz2-1.0.
Ubuntu source metadata: libarchive Build-Depends on libbz2-dev.
Ubuntu package description explicitly says libarchive supports reading and writing archives compressed with the bzip2 filter.
The installed header /usr/include/archive.h declares archive_read_support_filter_bzip2() and archive_write_add_filter_bzip2().

dependents.runtime_functionality: Supports on-the-fly decompression of bzip2-compressed PCF bitmap fonts through FreeType's BZIP2-enabled font loaders.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata: libfreetype6 depends on libbz2-1.0.
Ubuntu source metadata: freetype Build-Depends on libbz2-dev.
The installed header /usr/include/freetype2/freetype/config/ftoption.h says libbz2 is used to partially uncompress PCF files on the fly and defines FT_CONFIG_OPTION_USE_BZIP2.

dependents.runtime_functionality: Its Matroska/WebM plugin can decompress bzip2-compressed Matroska ContentCompression payloads while parsing streams.
evidence: Ubuntu package metadata: gstreamer1.0-plugins-good depends on libbz2-1.0.
Ubuntu source metadata: gst-plugins-good1.0 Build-Depends on libbz2-dev.
The installed plugin /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/gstreamer-1.0/libgstmatroska.so links libbz2 and contains BZ2_bzDecompress* symbols plus Matroska ContentCompression strings.
gst-inspect-1.0 matroska identifies that plugin as the Matroska and WebM stream handling module.
